hooks.prompt:
You are about to write or edit a source file. The following core rules apply based on the file extension. You MUST follow the relevant rules for your file type.

## 🔴 Core Coding Rules (must follow)

### Java
1. Use @Getter @AllArgsConstructor for event/POJO classes; NEVER use @Data
2. NEVER use @Autowired field injection; always use constructor injection (@RequiredArgsConstructor)
3. Use abstract method instead of instanceof chain for type dispatch
4. Use formatted() / Text Block for string formatting; never use + concatenation
5. Use @RequiredArgsConstructor for Service/Controller; @Getter @NoArgsConstructor(access = PROTECTED) for Entity

### Python
1. Use f-strings; never use % or .format()
2. snake_case for functions/variables; PascalCase for classes
3. Always annotate return types and parameter types (mypy must pass)
4. Use is / is not for None comparison; never use == None
5. Use Enum / Protocol / abstract method instead of isinstance() chain

### TypeScript
1. Standalone + OnPush + inject() (Angular) / Function components + hooks (React) / <script setup lang="ts"> (Vue)
2. Use output() instead of @Output() (Angular) / custom hooks over lifecycle methods (React) / composables (Vue)
3. Use signal() / computed() for reactive state management
4. Use loadComponent for lazy-loaded routes
5. Use interface for Props / Entity types

## 📖 Full Reference
Load the reef-style-backend or reef-style-frontend skill for the complete coding standard.

## ✅ Self-Verification
Before writing code, first output `✅ [STYLE] confirmed {language} rules` to acknowledge you have read and will follow the rules above.
